hubs.impossibility_statement: Purchasing Power Parity (equal price levels across countries) is impossible when countries differ in relative productivity between tradable and non-tradable sectors. Higher-productivity countries systematically have higher price levels for non-tradables.
hubs.formal_source: COMPOSE(trade_equalization + internal_wage_equalization) → COMPLETE(absolute_PPP) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(accept_systematic_price_level_differences) | Trade equalizes tradable goods prices. Internal labor mobility equalizes wages across sectors within a country. If country A has higher tradable-sector productivity, its wages are higher. Its non-tradable prices (set by wages, not trade) are also higher. The composition of the price index differs from the composition of traded goods, so aggregate price levels diverge systematically with productivity.

hubs.impossibility_statement: For any sequence of nested finite-dimensional subspaces V_1 ⊂ V_2 ⊂ ... in a Banach space X, and any rate ε_n → 0, there exists an element f ∈ X whose best approximation error E_n(f) = inf_{g ∈ V_n} ||f-g|| decreases no faster than ε_n.
hubs.formal_source: COMPOSE(nested_subspace_approximation) → COMPLETE(guaranteed_fast_rate) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(restrict_to_smoothness_subclass) | The quotient space X/V_n has infinite dimension for each n. The best approximation map is a projection onto V_n, and the sequence of distances d(f, V_n) can be arranged to match any prescribed rate by constructing f as a series with carefully chosen coefficients in directions nearly orthogonal to each V_n.

hubs.impossibility_statement: For functions in the Sobolev space W^s_p([0,1]^d), the optimal approximation rate using n parameters is O(n^{-s/d}). As dimension d grows, exponentially many parameters are needed to maintain a fixed accuracy. No method escapes this for the full Sobolev class.
hubs.formal_source: COMPOSE(grid_sampling) → COMPLETE(accurate_high_dimensional_approximation) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(exploit_low_dimensional_structure_or_sparsity) | The ε-covering number of the unit ball in W^s_p([0,1]^d) grows as exp(c·(1/ε)^{d/s}). Any linear or nonlinear n-term approximation scheme must respect this metric entropy lower bound (Kolmogorov n-widths). The volume of the unit cube grows exponentially with d while the number of sample points grows polynomially.

hubs.hub_name: Impossibility Du Bois Reymond Fourier Divergence
hubs.impossibility_statement: There exists a continuous function on [0,2π] whose Fourier series diverges at a point. Pointwise convergence of Fourier series is not guaranteed even for continuous functions.
hubs.formal_source: COMPOSE(Fourier_partial_sums) → COMPLETE(pointwise_recovery) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(restrict_to_Lp_convergence_or_Cesaro_summation) | The Dirichlet kernel D_n has unbounded L^1 norm (grows as log n). By the Banach-Steinhaus (uniform boundedness) theorem, the set of continuous functions where S_n(f,x) diverges is residual (comeager) in C[0,2π]. The partial sum operator sequence is not uniformly bounded in the sup-norm.

hubs.impossibility_statement: For any triangular array of interpolation nodes in [a,b], there exists a continuous function for which the interpolating polynomial sequence diverges. No single choice of nodes guarantees convergence for ALL continuous functions.
hubs.formal_source: COMPOSE(polynomial_interpolation_on_fixed_nodes) → COMPLETE(convergence_for_all_f_in_C[a,b]) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(restrict_smoothness_class_or_use_best_approximation) | The Banach-Steinhaus theorem applies: the interpolation operators L_n: C[a,b]→C[a,b] are bounded linear. The Lebesgue constant Λ_n = ||L_n|| grows without bound for ANY node array (Λ_n ≥ (2/π)log(n+1) - C). By uniform boundedness, pointwise divergence occurs on a residual set.

hubs.impossibility_statement: The span of monomials {x^{λ_n}} is dense in C[0,1] if and only if Σ 1/λ_n = ∞. If the exponents are too sparse (series converges), the monomial system cannot approximate all continuous functions.
hubs.formal_source: COMPOSE(lacunary_monomial_span) → COMPLETE(dense_in_C[0,1]) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(add_missing_exponents_until_divergence_condition_met) | When Σ 1/λ_n < ∞, the closed span of {x^{λ_n}} in C[0,1] is a proper subspace. The dual characterization shows there exist nonzero measures orthogonal to all x^{λ_n}. The missing monomials create gaps in the frequency coverage that cannot be compensated by the remaining terms.

hubs.impossibility_statement: For a SISO feedback system, the complementary sensitivity T(s) = 1 - S(s) satisfies its own integral constraint: for plants with time delay tau, integral_0^inf (1/omega^2) * ln|T(j*omega)| d_omega <= pi*tau/2. Tracking (requiring T close to 1) and noise rejection (requiring T close to 0) at high frequencies cannot both be achieved. The time delay tau sets an absolute limit: no controller can make the closed-loop bandwidth exceed approximately 1/tau while maintaining stability margins.
hubs.formal_source: COMPOSE(tracking + noise rejection + time delay) -> COMPLETE(both perfect) FAILS -> BREAK_SYMMETRY(limit bandwidth or accept noise amplification)

hubs.impossibility_statement: No approximation scheme can achieve a universal rate of convergence for all continuous functions simultaneously. For any sequence of approximation operators L_n with dim(L_n)=n, there exist continuous functions that converge arbitrarily slowly.
hubs.formal_source: COMPOSE(finite_basis_expansion) → COMPLETE(uniform_rate_for_all_f) FAILS → BREAK_SYMMETRY(restrict_function_class_by_smoothness_or_spectral_decay) | By the Bernstein lethargy theorem, for any decreasing sequence ε_n→0, there exists f in the target space such that the best approximation error E_n(f) decreases no faster than ε_n. The unit ball in C[a,b] is not compact, so no uniform rate covers all elements.

hubs.impossibility_statement: In Valiant's (2009) formal model of evolvability, evolution is modeled as a restricted form of statistical query learning. A concept class is evolvable iff it is learnable by statistical queries with tolerance. Boolean functions requiring superpolynomial statistical queries (e.g., parities, cryptographic functions) are provably not evolvable in polynomial generations. Evolution cannot discover arbitrary Boolean functions; it is strictly weaker than PAC learning.
hubs.formal_source: COMPOSE(mutation + selection + polynomial time) -> COMPLETE(arbitrary function learning) FAILS -> BREAK_SYMMETRY(restrict target class or allow exponential time)

hubs.impossibility_statement: Zames (1981) formulated the H-infinity optimal control problem: minimize ||W*S||_inf over all stabilizing controllers, where W is a performance weighting and S is the sensitivity function. The minimum achievable value gamma_opt > 0 is determined by the plant's unstable poles and zeros via Nevanlinna-Pick interpolation. For a plant with RHP pole p and RHP zero z, gamma_opt >= |p + conj(z)|/|p - z|. No controller can reduce weighted sensitivity below this plant-intrinsic bound.
hubs.formal_source: COMPOSE(sensitivity minimization + stability + unstable plant) -> COMPLETE(arbitrarily low sensitivity) FAILS -> BREAK_SYMMETRY(accept gamma_opt floor or redesign plant)
